title: 楚辞 · Songs of Chu
desc: The shamanistic southern voice that gave Chinese literature its second pole — visionary, romantic, and inconsolably exiled.

title: 昆曲 · Kun Opera
desc: The oldest surviving form of Chinese opera — refined to such perfection over four centuries that it nearly disappeared from its own beauty.

title: 鲁迅 · Lu Xun
desc: The writer who looked at traditional Chinese society and saw cannibalism — and the complicated, unresolvable legacy he left behind.

title: 诗经 · Book of Songs
desc: China oldest poetry anthology — three hundred poems that Confucius said no educated person could afford to ignore.

title: 水墨画 · Ink-Wash Painting
desc: Where the brush meets philosophy — five tones of ink, the art of leaving space, and landscapes that are not about landscape at all.

title: 宋词 · Song Dynasty Lyric
desc: The poem that sings — fixed tune patterns, named meters, and three masters who defined what the Chinese lyric could feel.

title: 文房四宝 · Four Treasures of the Study
desc: Brush, ink, inkstone, and paper — the four instruments through which a scholar thought, wrote, painted, and demonstrated who he was.

title: 篆刻 · Seal Carving
desc: The art of the name — bronze to stone, dynasty to dynasty, two schools of carvers, and the small red square that authenticates everything.

title: 佛 · Buddha, the awakened
desc: The character that carried an entire worldview across the Silk Road — etymology, Buddhism, the 佛-family of compounds.

title: 角 · corner, horn, angle
desc: Corner, horn, angle — the character that names this field guide.

title: 落 · to fall, to settle, to land
desc: Falling, settling, and the beauty of things arriving at their place.

title: 木 · tree, wood
desc: A tree seen whole: trunk, branches, roots. The pictograph that became a radical, a wuxing element, and the building block of 林, 森, 本, and 末.

title: 书 · book, writing, letter
desc: Book, writing, calligraphy — the character that holds civilization together.

title: 水 · water
desc: The first of the five elements, the radical that runs through hundreds of compounds, and the Daoist image of how to act in the world.

title: 王 · king
desc: Three horizontals joined by one vertical — the ruler who connects heaven, earth, and humanity. Also the most common Chinese surname, and a radical that is secretly 玉 (jade) in disguise.

title: 屋 · room, house, dwelling
desc: Room, dwelling, and the human need for shelter — the character at home.

title: 要 · to want, must, essential
desc: Want, need, must, and will — the grammar engine disguised as a single character.

title: 再 · again, then, further
desc: Again, one more time, and the subtle art of "and then" — the character that sequences and repeats.

title: 在 · to be at, located, in progress
desc: Location, existence, and the progressive aspect — the character that puts things in place and actions in motion.

title: 画蛇添足 · Drawing Legs on a Snake
desc: He finished first, so he kept drawing — and lost the prize. The idiom for ruining a good thing by overdoing it.
